name: Neramexane
description: Neramexane is a low-to-moderate affinity uncompetitive NMDA receptor antagonist.
indication: Investigated for use/treatment in alzheimer's disease, hearing loss, and pain (acute or chronic).
mechanismOfAction: Neramexane is an uncompetitive NMDA receptor channel blocker.
pharmacodynamics: Neramexane is similar to memantine and acetylcholinesterase inhibitors in that neramexane targets the cognitive decline in Alzheimer’s Disease by altering neurotransmitter signaling, but not the underlying pathological mechanisms that cause the disease (amyloid production or neurofibrillary tangle accumulation).
